children.text: first establishing, through a procedure the Attorney General by regulation prescribes in consultation with the Secretary of Transportation under , that the major part has not been reported as stolen; andsection 33109 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: identifying the vehicle identification number (or derivative of that number) of that major part; and

children.label: B
children.text: verifying that the major part has not been reported as stolen.

children.label: A
children.text: is the manufacturer of the major part;

children.label: B
children.text: has purchased the major part directly from the manufacturer; or

children.label: C
children.text: has received a verification from an insurance carrier under that the motor vehicle from which the major part is derived has not been reported as stolen, or that the carrier has not established whether that vehicle has been stolen.section 33110 of this title

A person described under paragraph (1)(C) of this subsection that subsequently transfers or sells in commerce the motor vehicle or a major part of the vehicle shall provide the verification received from the carrier to the person to whom the vehicle or part is transferred or sold.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: c .—Regulations
children.text: The Attorney General shall prescribe regulations to carry out this section. The regulations shall include regulations prescribed in consultation with the Secretary that are necessary to ensure that a verification a person provides under subsection (a)(2) of this section is uniform, effective, and resistant to fraudulent use.
